title: challenge started
detail: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

payload.message: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

title: First model counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 12000 cents → REFUND; inventory 10/0 → 10/1

counterexample.title: counterexample-standard-damaged-12000
counterexample.rationale: High: one minimally changed crossed observation separates STANDARD-tier control from the competing at-or-above-12000 amount rule while also testing whether 12000 cents is behaviorally significant.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crossed input holds the previously observed 12000-cent DAMAGED case constant while changing VIP to STANDARD, making the tier-driven and at-or-above-12000 amount-driven explanations prescribe different decisions. Using exactly 12000 cents directly probes the proposed inclusive amount boundary without introducing a new amount value.

title: High-information counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: counterexample-standard-damaged-75000
counterexample.rationale: Very high: this single high-value DAMAGED input tests whether the fresh STANDARD refund behavior persists at a materially larger amount, whether a high-value priority exception supersedes automatic processing, and—because all existing DAMAGED traces moved one unit to quarantine during processing—whether that inventory movement remains immediate or is deferred under the exception.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: A STANDARD DAMAGED return at 75000 cents preserves the crossed tier-versus-amount conflict while moving materially above every automatically processed trace, exposing any higher-value priority exception. The 75000-cent amount tests whether amount can override the freshly observed STANDARD-tier behavior beyond the previously exercised 12000-cent value.

title: Host narrowed the exact threshold
detail: 49999 cents processes automatically; 50000 cents enters review.

title: Host priority counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · priority at the boundary
counterexample.rationale: The host crosses customer tier at the exact review boundary to distinguish threshold priority from VIP replacement.

title: Host bounded the covered high-value interval
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

title: Contract rule · priority 10
detail: Priority 10: Within the observed DAMAGED domain for STANDARD or VIP customers, amountCents at or above exactly 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no refund, no inventory change, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue effect.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 10: Within the observed DAMAGED domain for STANDARD or VIP customers, amountCents at or above exactly 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no refund, no inventory change, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue effect.

title: Contract rule · priority 20
detail: Priority 20: For the observed VIP+DAMAGED sub-threshold point at 12000 cents, produce REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and issue a one-unit shipment; behavior at other VIP amounts below 50000 remains unknown.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 20: For the observed VIP+DAMAGED sub-threshold point at 12000 cents, produce REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and issue a one-unit shipment; behavior at other VIP amounts below 50000 remains unknown.

title: Contract rule · priority 30
detail: Priority 30: Within the observed STANDARD+DAMAGED domain, amountCents below exactly 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine plus one, and a refund-ledger effect.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 30: Within the observed STANDARD+DAMAGED domain, amountCents below exactly 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine plus one, and a refund-ledger effect.
